prompt: hey can you mock up a 'License usage' page for me? it's a dashboard view where customers can see how many SDK licenses they're using vs their quota. think: page heading, then 3 stat cards across the top (total licenses, in use, available), then a table below listing each license with columns for license key, product, status, last used. status should have colored badges (active = green, expired = red, pending = yellow). just one standalone html file, nothing fancy. our design lead is going to look at it tomorrow so make sure it actually looks like scandit, not generic admin template

assertions.text: Uses Inter font (declared in CSS or loaded via CDN)
No raw hex colors in product UI markup (hex only appears inside a :root / token-definition block, never in inline styles or arbitrary tailwind classes like bg-[#...])
Uses tinted neutrals — avoids #000000 / #FFFFFF as text or background outside of legitimate background/black or border/white usages

Status badges use Scandit-aligned green / red / yellow that match the design tokens (green ≈ #0D853D, red ≈ #D92121, yellow ≈ #FFD300 or matching ramp values), not generic Tailwind green-500/red-500/yellow-400
Renders 3 stat cards across the top with the requested labels (total licenses, in use, available)
Renders a table with columns: license key, product, status, last used
Layout feels dense and information-rich (not airy with huge whitespace and oversized type), consistent with the Scandit dashboard aesthetic
Card corner radius is consistent with system (16px / rounded-2xl), button radius 8px, no overly rounded marketing-style shapes
Shadows are ambient and pure-vertical at low opacity (consistent with shadow / shadow-md tokens), not directional drop shadows with offsets
